name: Wood
description: A basic material for shipbuilding and rigging. Lets you patch the hull at sea. Found everywhere and one of the main resources in ports. Can also be obtained from #sawmills or by #dismantling captured ships. Could the whole Archipelago be deforested...?

name: Rum
description: Rum can be used to dilute water and extend its storage - or simply to lift the mood... That's why sailors ain't too fond of strict discipline, get it? Often paid instead of wages, for example to craftsmen when building cannons.

name: Fabric
description: Mostly made from hemp or flax. Best source is capturing and #breaking down ships for sailcloth, or spinning wool from #animals into fabric.

name: Iron
description: Foundation for construction and casting, especially weaponry. Obtained by processing iron ore from #mines or #smelting old cannons in the workshop. Merchant ships often carry iron.

name: Water
description: Crystal-clear freshwater (for now) from local glaciers. Seawater, of course, isn’t good for drinking or cooking... Cities rely on #water supply managed by the port-owning faction. Without it, cities face shortages. Water can be freely traded at the auction or smuggled to certain bays.

name: Sugar
description: A food commodity with demand varying by region. Can be #sold in settlements and to sea traders. Sugar is also used for rum production.

name: Beer
description: A drink that's quickly gaining popularity, though not as favored as sailors' rum yet. Beer brine helps preserve things, making it valuable to settlements and sea merchants.

name: Captives
description: In the Archipelago, the slave trade is prohibited. Yet, it flourishes in pirate ports, where captives work in #mines and #factories, serving as servants on #personal islands.

name: Supplies
description: Fruits, tobacco, coffee, and spices are highly prized across the Archipelago and are vital ingredients in #provisions. They can be discovered while #exploring #islands

name: Grain
description: In demand on #farms and settlements due to its relative cheapness. Often available from sea merchants. Nine happy rats in the hold out of ten!

name: Copper
description: Crucial for alloys and #weapon production. Only gained by processing copper ore from #mines or #melting down old weapons in the workshop.

name: Coal
description: Irreplaceable for iron forging, cannon casting, and heating northern settlements. Found in #coal mines or made by #burning wrecks in the workshop. Very expensive in cold regions; in the south, kids use it instead of snowballs.

name: Resin
description: Pitch coating protects parts from moisture and makes the workers sticky. Produced at special #productions.

name: Bronze
description: Durable alloy made in the #workshop for high-quality #weapons and #upgrades. Bronze ingots are heavy enough to be parts or melee weapons.

name: Escudo
description: This is what the imperial currency is called in the Archipelago. Only these gold coins are accepted when building Imperial ships. They can also be traded for valuables at #altars of the “sea gods,” where local natives will gladly make a deal.

name: Plate
description: A large iron part. Produced in the #workshop. Required for building large #ships and #fortifications.

name: Canvas
description: Thick, heavy fabric resistant to moisture, made in the #workshop. A strategic resource used, oddly enough, for sail making and repair.

name: Resin
description: Coating parts with resin protects them from moisture and makes the ones who applied it sticky. Produced in specialized facilities at sea.

name: Cargo Crate
description: Quest item

name: Wreckage
description: What's left floating after a ship is destroyed. Can be burned into #coal in the workshop.

name: Fresh Meat
description: Unlike rotten meat, this can be used for cooking. Part of #rations and hearty meals.

name: Animals
description: Transporting animals is profitable but requires preparation. You'll also need to clean the hold and deck... The animals survive long voyages and provide #meat and #wool for clothing.

name: Volcanic Ore
description: Scattered across the #islands like an echo of the primeval volcanic eruption. Can be refined into iron or used in #cannon casting. Barrels with volcanic ore in the alloy are unmatched in quality

name: Chest
description: An excellent way to store your #savings safely. To get the gold out… just open the chest in port. Even a small gold chest makes a great piece of decor.

name: Amber
description: A unique wax-like substance highly valued by perfumers and medics of the Khanfar Caliphate. The only commodity that can be traded at auction during the #Tender. Disappears from all warehouses after the Tender ends!

name: removed
description: removed

name: Beam
description: Made in the #workshop through woodcrafting. Versatile and used in building #ships and creating their #upgrades.

name: Bulkhead
description: Large iron part made in the workshop for #ship construction. You better secure it properly during transport, especially in storms, to not send your crew flying to Davy Jones' locker...

name: Antiquities
description: Captain's silverware and pretty accessories. May be a bit rusty, but still valuable to some collectors or for #selling to #traders. Can be #melted in port to get copper.

name: Iron Ore
description: Mined from iron #mines. Smelted into #iron in the workshop.

name: Copper Ore
description: Mined from copper #mines. Can be turned into #copper at the workshop.

name: Insurance
description: This fancy-looking piece of paper lets you build any top-level mine or manufactory for free or upgrade the port warehouse to the fifth level.

name: Fish
description: Freshly caught and cooked fish is a delicacy for sailors with a poor #diet. Often sold in ports, but best exchanged with special traders for rare goods.

name: Scrolls
description: Needed for #mode access. Find scrolls at sea or earn them through #quests and arena battles.

name: Tackles
description: Can be crafted in the #workshop or “borrowed” from fishermen. If tackle is in your hold, fishing spots will be marked on the map. Tackle significantly #boosts your #catch and allows harpooning whales near your ship. There's more fish and whales up north.

name: Battle Mark
description: This currency lets you buy #rare #items from merchants and get approval from the admiralty for building certain ships or setting up new production. You can earn it through Arena and sea battles. Beware that trading it at an auction comes with extra taxes.

name: Voodoo Skull
description: An unusual find that merchants can exchange for #rare #items and upgrades. Whether it's a real skull or not, one can only guess.

name: Provision
description: Food supplies for sailors and workers on the land, serving far from cities.

name: Construction License
description: This document lets you #upgrade a #fort to the highest level, instantly repair all structures, or build a specific ship.

name: Modification Blueprint
description: Use this blueprint to let the shipyard masters modify an existing upgrade or install a new one, including rare ones.

name: Chest Key
description: Mysterious key. Collect them to open a chest and get rewards.

name: Saffron
description: A spice valued as highly as gold. It's also used as a dye for the clothing of the Archipelago's aristocrats.

name: Paprika
description: A sweet and mildly spicy seasoning made from special peppers. Used in many popular dishes. For example, the famous Laguno-Blanco omelet wouldn't be the same without paprika.

name: Cinnamon
description: Used as a fragrant spice and flavor enhancer.

name: Salt
description: Essential everywhere, even though it's plentiful on the Archipelago. Salted meat can last at "room" temperature and is crucial for long voyages.

name: Silk
description: Valued for its exquisite qualities and beauty. Only the wealthiest of the Archipelago can afford it. Trading silk is a path to riches.

name: Rugs
description: It's a treasure you can't walk on!

name: Dates
description: Edible fruits from certain types of palm trees, usually eaten dried.

name: Mango
description: Very sweet and nutritious.

name: Tobacco
description: Despite Bridgeport's medical warnings about the dangers of smoking pipes, tobacco's popularity and trade volume keep rising.

name: Coffee
description: Beans for brewing the most popular hot drink. Coffee's so essential, it keeps you from falling off the mast and breaking your neck in the middle of the night! It's not grown on the Archipelago, so we might run out one day...

name: Leather
description: Durable, flexible, and long-lasting material.

name: Pepper
description: Goes great with almost anything, be it meat, poultry, or vegetables. Even if the cook's skills are as rough as the high seas, a pinch of pepper can save the day.

name: Vanilla
description: A sweet spice, highly prized for the laborious process of cultivating vanilla pods.

name: Nuts
description: Nutritious and long-lasting

name: Wine
description: A sophisticated alcoholic drink. Winemaking and wine drinking are arts in themselves, much like the trade of fine wine.

name: Pineapples
description: Fresh fruit on board is a rarity on long voyages. Highly prized, they help prevent scurvy and other diseases from lack of vitamins.

name: Oil
description: Essential for cooking, making incense, and producing medicines.

name: Grog
description: Watered-down rum with lime and cinnamon. Lasts longer than plain water, prevents illness, and brightens a sailor's hard life.

name: Blueprint Fragment
description: Some ships can only be built using unique Imperial blueprints. You can find blueprint fragments by sinking Imperial flagships, and once you’ve studied enough, you can build a unique ship.

name: Imperial Blueprint
description: Collect #fragments and visit a ragpicker to assemble a complete blueprint. The ragpicker can be found in ports (Workshop section) and charge a hefty fee for their services!

name: Seahorse
description: These remarkable little fish, shaped like a chess knight, are in high demand among overseas merchants. Some say it’s due to a fashion among Khanfar nobles to keep them in aquariums... or perhaps it’s tied to the coming Year of the Horse.

name: Whale Oil
description: The main fuel for lamps and #lanterns, lighting up the darkest corners of the Archipelago. Ships carrying it can easily be found by their smell.

name: Mysterious Map
description: Collect all parts of the mysterious map to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) on the map where #treasure is hidden.

name: Pirate Token
description: A special currency for "insiders." Only it can be used to get certain services from pirates... Can be found by sinking pirates and Order ships. Pirate bays have a trader willing to exchange tokens for rare items

name: Merchant Supply Map
description: Collect all map parts to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) showing where #supplies are hidden. Pieces are found by sinking trade ships.

name: Pirate Treasure Map
description: Collect all map pieces to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) showing where the #treasure is hidden. Map fragments are found by sinking elite ships with a star.

name: Fanatic Camp Map
description: Collect all map parts to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) showing the location of a #camp. Pieces are found by sinking fanatic ships.
